case_title: BILL MOUNSEY et al VS. SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY (SFM et al

docket_entries.description: MINUTE ORDER RE: DENYING WITHOUT PREJUDICE PLAINTIFFS' APPLICATION FOR PUBLICATION OF SUMMONS

LAW AND MOTION 302, NO APPEARANCE ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY (SFMTA) EDWARD D. REISKIN, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS AND CRISTINA RUBKE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO JUNE 18, 2013. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT, NOT REPORTED.

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - JUDGE MILLER MADE A DISCLOSURE REGARDING CRISTINA RUPKE WHO IN AN EMPLOYEE WHERE HIS HUSBAND IS A PARTNER. SHE HAVE MET HER A FEW TIMES OVER THE YEARS THAT SHE WORKED THERE, BUT SHE DOES NOT KNOW HER PERSONALLY. SHE HAVE NEVER TALKED TO HER ABOUT HER SERVICE ON THE MTA COMMISSION OR ABOUT THIS CASE. SHE ALSO DISCLOSED REGARDING ANN LAZARUS IS A SOCIAL FRIEND. SHE SEE HER ABOUT ONCE A YEAR. SHE ASSUMES THAT THIS IS THE SAME ANN LAZARUS WHOSE NAME APPEARS ON THE PLEADING IS THE CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF APPEALS, AND SHE MADE THIS ASSUMPTION ONLY BECAUSE THE ANN LAZARUS THAT SHE KNOWS IS INVOLVED IN CIVIC MATTERS. SHE DID NOT KNOW THIS UNTIL SEEING THE DEMURRER THAT SOMEONE NAMED ANN LAZARUS WAS CHAIR OF THE BOARD OF APPEALS. SHE NEVER DISCUSEED IT WITH HER. THIS DOES NOT AFFECT HER ABILITY TO BE FAIR AND IMPARTIAL. THE COURT GAVE COUNSEL OPPORTUNITY TO ASK QUESTION. THERE WERE NO OBJECTIONS OR QUESTIONS RAISED. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, EDWARD D. REISKIN, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS AND CRISTINA RUBKE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITHOUT PREJUDICE FOR THE CITY TO RAISE THE ISSUE IN ANOTHER CONTEXT. THE COURT FINDS THAT THE CITY HAS NOT MET ITS BURDEN. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUL-10-2013 CONTINUED TO AUG-21-2013 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

Robert F. Kane, Esq. of the Law Offices of Robert F. Kane, Tel. 415.982.1510, for plaintiff and opposing party.

REISKIN, IN HIS OFFICIAL CAPACITY AS, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN IN HER OFFICIAL CAPACITY, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS and CRISTINA RUBKE's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT or, in the alternative, Summary Adjudication is continued to September 22, 2014. No appearance is required on that date. Plaintiff's counsel is to file additional brief/declaration no later than August 27, and defendant's counsel may file a responsive brief no later than September 4. Judge: Ernest H. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-25-2013 Continued To Aug-21-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT F. KANE, TEL. NO. 982-1510 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4675 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - Judge Miller made a disclosure regarding Cristina Rupke who in an employee where his husband is a partner. She have met her a few times over the years that she worked there, but she does not know her personally. She have never talked to her about her service on the MTA Commission or about this case. She also disclosed regarding Ann Lazarus is a social friend. She see her about once a year. She assumes that this is the same Ann Lazarus whose name appears on the pleading is the chair of the Board of Appeals, and she made this assumption only because the Ann Lazarus that she knows is involved in civic matters. She did not know this until seeing the demurrer that someone named Ann Lazarus was chair of the Board of Appeals. She never discuseed it with her. This does not affect her ability to be fair and impartial. The Court gave counsel opportunity to ask question. There were no objections or question raised. The matter was argued. DEFENDANTs SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, EDWARD D. REISKIN, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS and CRISTINA RUBKE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is Denied. The court finds that the City has not met its burden. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-18-2013 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. ROBERT F. KANE, TEL. NO. 982-1510 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4675 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENTS.

RULING - Judge Miller made a disclosure regarding Cristina Rupke who in an employee where his husband is a partner. She have met her a few times over the years that she worked there, but she does not know her personally. She have never talked to her about her service on the MTA Commission or about this case. She also disclosed regarding Ann Lazarus is a social friend. She see her about once a year. She assumes that this is the same Ann Lazarus whose name appears on the pleading is the chair of the Board of Appeals, and she made this assumption only because the Ann Lazarus that she knows is involved in civic matters. She did not know this until seeing the demurrer that someone named Ann Lazarus was chair of the Board of Appeals. She never discuseed it with her. This does not affect her ability to be fair and impartial. The Court gave counsel opportunity to ask question. There were no objections or questions raised. The matter was argued. DEFENDANTS SAN FRANCISCO MUNICIPAL TRANSPORTATION AGENCY, EDWARD D. REISKIN, TOM NOLAN, CHERYL BRINKMAN, MALCOLM HEINICKE, JERRY LEE, LEONA BRIDGES, JOEL RAMOS and CRISTINA RUBKE'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled without prejudice for the city to raise the issue in another context. The court finds that the City has not met its burden. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185
